sourceMovie.title: Prometheus
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Prometheus (2012) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Ridley Scott's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Thing
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Prometheus clicked for you, The Thing (1982) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Event Horizon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Prometheus clicked for you, Event Horizon (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Prometheus?
faq.answer: Try The Thing, Event Horizon, The Thing—each shares DNA with Prometheus while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Prometheus?
faq.answer: Prometheus (2012) is best described as Science Fiction, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Prometheus part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Prometheus Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
